game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Meridian 157: Kapitel 1** Players navigate a mysterious subarctic facility as David Zander in a first-person point-and-click adventure. Movement between static scenes is achieved by tapping directional arrows displayed on screen, while interacting with environmental objects or collecting items involves tapping specific hotspots. Puzzles range from inventory-based combinations, where collected items are dragged from a bottom-screen inventory onto relevant scene elements, to logic puzzles requiring players to decipher codes or manipulate mechanisms with 3-5 distinct steps. Progression is linear, with each solved puzzle unlocking new areas or advancing the narrative, and a contextual hint system is available after a 60-second cooldown for each progressive clue. The game concludes upon solving the overarching mystery of Meridian 157, with no failure conditions beyond being unable to solve a puzzle.
game_info.description: You are David Zander investigating the mysterious Meridian 157 subarctic facility. Navigate through static scenes, collect items, and solve puzzles to uncover the facility's secrets. Use inventory items on hotspots, decipher codes, and manipulate mechanisms to progress. Each solved puzzle unlocks new areas. The mystery concludes when you solve the final puzzle revealing the truth about Meridian 157.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between scenes (when arrows are visible)
Space: Interact with hotspots / Use selected inventory item
Z: Select/cycle through inventory items
Shift: Request hint (60-second cooldown between hints)
Enter: Start game
Esc: Pause/unpause
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Basic navigation and interaction testing. Tests if the player can move between scenes using directional navigation and interact with basic hotspots in the environment.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move through available scenes in sequence, interact with obvious hotspots, and collect any visible items. Tests basic movement and interaction mechanics without attempting to solve complex puzzles.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Successfully navigate through at least 3 different scenes, interact with at least 2 hotspots, and collect at least 1 item. Test is successful if these actions complete without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Complete playthrough testing to win the game. Tests the full puzzle-solving sequence from start to finish, ensuring all mechanics work together to reach the win condition.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Follow the optimal puzzle solution path: collect key items in correct order, use items on appropriate hotspots, solve logic puzzles with correct inputs, and progress through all areas systematically until reaching the final puzzle.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Reach GAME_OVER_WIN state by solving all puzzles in the correct sequence. Success is achieving the win condition within a reasonable number of moves (under 200 actions).

TEST_3.test_description: Inventory management testing. Tests the ability to collect multiple items, cycle through inventory, and use items on correct scene elements.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Systematically collect all available items, cycle through inventory using Z key, and attempt to use each item on various hotspots to verify inventory system works correctly.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Successfully collect all items, cycle through inventory without errors, and successfully use at least one item on a hotspot. Inventory state should update correctly after each action.
